expectations.text: Creates a HullComponent with at least 8 BuoyancySamplePoints distributed across the hull
expectations.evidence: FishingBoatFactory creates 16 sample points across bow, forward quarter, aft quarter, stern

expectations.text: BuoyancySystem transforms sample points to world space and queries wave height using galaxy-space coordinates
expectations.evidence: Transforms via worldPt.set(sp.localOffset).mul(worldTx), converts to galaxy-space doubles via originManager

expectations.text: Buoyancy force direction uses hull normal (not just world-up) to produce natural restoring torques
expectations.evidence: Force set from sp.normal rotated into world space, torque computed as cross product of lever arm and force

expectations.text: HydrodynamicDragSystem subtracts water current velocity before computing drag
expectations.evidence: relVel.set(linearVel).sub(waterBody.currentVelocity)

expectations.text: Drag is quadratic in speed (uses v² term)
expectations.evidence: Uses v * |v| pattern for sign-preserving v-squared

expectations.text: All forces applied via btRigidBody.applyCentralForce/applyTorque, not direct velocity manipulation
expectations.evidence: Both systems exclusively use applyCentralForce and applyTorque

expectations.text: Uses Pools.obtain/free for temporary Vector3 objects
expectations.evidence: Both BuoyancySystem and HydrodynamicDragSystem use Pools.obtain/free

expectations.text: Does not hardcode water density — reads from WaterBodyComponent
expectations.evidence: Both systems read waterBody.density from WaterBodyComponent

expectations.text: Creates a HullComponent with at least 8 BuoyancySamplePoints distributed across the hull

expectations.text: BuoyancySystem transforms sample points to world space and queries wave height using galaxy-space coordinates
expectations.evidence: Transforms via matrix multiplication, converts to galaxy-space via CoordinateManager

expectations.text: Buoyancy force direction uses hull normal (not just world-up) to produce natural restoring torques
expectations.evidence: Force applied along world-space hull normal via applyForce(force, relPos)

expectations.text: HydrodynamicDragSystem subtracts water current velocity before computing drag
expectations.evidence: relVel = linearVel - waterBody.currentVelocity

expectations.text: Drag is quadratic in speed (uses v² term)
expectations.evidence: Uses v * |v| for all three axes plus angular damping

expectations.text: All forces applied via btRigidBody.applyCentralForce/applyTorque, not direct velocity manipulation
expectations.evidence: All systems use applyForce, applyCentralForce, applyTorque exclusively

expectations.text: Uses Pools.obtain/free for temporary Vector3 objects

expectations.text: Does not hardcode water density — reads from WaterBodyComponent
expectations.evidence: Density always read from WaterBodyComponent.density

expectations.text: BallastComponent has PID gains (Kp, Ki, Kd) and BallastTanks with capacity/fill/rates
expectations.evidence: depthKp, depthKi, depthKd fields plus Array<BallastTank> with capacity, currentFill, fillRate, drainRate

expectations.text: BallastSystem implements PID controller: proportional + integral + derivative terms for depth error
expectations.evidence: Full P+I+D computation with integral clamping and dead-band

expectations.text: Ballast mass changes update rigid body via setMassProps()
expectations.evidence: physBody.body.setMassProps(hull.dryMass + ballastMass, inertia) called each tick

expectations.text: Hull crush depth triggers a HullBreachEvent via EventBus
expectations.evidence: eventBus.publish(new SubmarineHullBreachEvent(...)) when currentDepth > hull.crushDepth

expectations.text: FloodingSystem uses Torricelli's theorem (flow proportional to sqrt of head) for breach ingress
expectations.evidence: flow = Cd * A * sqrt(2*g*h) in FloodingSystem Phase 1

expectations.text: Cross-compartment flow equalizes based on water head difference between connected compartments
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 computes head difference and transfers proportional to sqrt(2*g*|headDiff|)

expectations.text: Free surface effect is computed for partially-flooded compartments
expectations.evidence: computeFreeSurfaceEffect() calculates GZ_loss = rho * I_free / displacement for partial fill

expectations.text: Flooding shifts the rigid body's centre of mass, not just its total mass
expectations.evidence: floodedCoM computed correctly but adjustRigidBodyMass() only calls setMassProps, never applies CoM offset to body

expectations.text: BallastComponent has PID gains (Kp, Ki, Kd) and BallastTanks with capacity/fill/rates
expectations.evidence: PID gains in DepthControlComponent; BallastTankComponent has capacity, currentVolume, flowRate

expectations.text: BallastSystem implements PID controller: proportional + integral + derivative terms for depth error
expectations.evidence: DepthControlSystem computes P+I+D with anti-windup

expectations.text: Ballast mass changes update rigid body via setMassProps()
expectations.evidence: No setMassProps() call anywhere. Uses force-based compensation instead

expectations.text: Hull crush depth triggers a HullBreachEvent via EventBus
expectations.evidence: HullIntegritySystem publishes HullBreachEvent when integrity drops below threshold

expectations.text: FloodingSystem uses Torricelli's theorem (flow proportional to sqrt of head) for breach ingress
expectations.evidence: Uses linear pressure scaling (1 + depth/100), not sqrt(2*g*h). No sqrt() in flooding code

expectations.text: Cross-compartment flow equalizes based on water head difference between connected compartments
expectations.evidence: Computes levelDiff between compartments and transfers proportionally

expectations.text: Free surface effect is computed for partially-flooded compartments
expectations.evidence: No free surface effect computation. Metacentric height is static 1.5f

expectations.text: Flooding shifts the rigid body's centre of mass, not just its total mass
expectations.evidence: Applies torques to simulate effect but never calls setCenterOfMassTransform

expectations.text: At least 3 compartments defined with connections between them
expectations.evidence: FloodableShipFactory creates engine_room, cargo_hold, crew_quarters with DoorwayConnections

expectations.evidence: applyTorpedoBreach() sets both fields on engine room

expectations.text: Breach ingress uses discharge coefficient (Cd ~0.6) and Torricelli's theorem
expectations.evidence: BREACH_CD = 0.6f, flow = Cd * A * sqrt(2*g*h)

expectations.text: Cross-compartment flow uses head difference and orifice equation
expectations.evidence: simulateCrossFlow() computes head diff, applies Q = Cd * A * sqrt(2*g*|dh|)

expectations.text: Flooded mass and centre-of-mass shift are computed and applied to the rigid body
expectations.evidence: updateFloodedMassAndCoM() computes weighted centroid, applies via applyCentralForce/applyTorque

expectations.evidence: computeFreeSurfaceEffect() with parabolic envelope peaking at 50% fill

expectations.text: StabilityWarningEvent published when GZ loss exceeds threshold

expectations.text: CapsizeEvent published when roll exceeds critical angle
expectations.evidence: checkCapsize() publishes when roll > 60 degrees for 2+ seconds

expectations.text: Event-driven HUD integration — subscribes to stability/flooding events, does not poll
expectations.evidence: FloodingHudSystem subscribes via eventBus.subscribe(), trigger-based not polling

notes: Eval 1 (fishing-boat-waves) assertions pass 100% in both configurations — non-discriminating. The model already knows buoyancy physics well enough to pass these checks without the skill.
Eval 3 (torpedo-flooding-capsize) also passes 100% in both — non-discriminating. The prompt is specific enough about Torricelli/capsize that the model implements them correctly either way.

Eval 2 (submarine-ballast-depth) is the discriminating eval: 88% with skill vs 50% without. Without the skill, the model missed Torricelli flow (used linear scaling), free surface effect entirely, and setMassProps().
The skill adds ~163s average execution time and ~22k tokens per run — moderate overhead for physics fidelity gains.
Both with-skill and without-skill failed to actually apply CoM offset to the Bullet rigid body (assertion 8 in eval 2). The skill should add explicit code for setCenterOfMassTransform().

Qualitative benefit not captured by assertions: with-skill runs use consistent component naming (HullComponent, BallastComponent) and system priority ordering (1-5), while without-skill runs invent their own naming conventions.
